response.justification: Wei Chen is an accomplished full-stack engineer with 15+ years of experience and strong software craftsmanship practices, but lacks essential qualifications for this C++ systems role. His C++ exposure is minimal and dated (6 months at National Instruments in 2014–2015), while his recent 10-year career has focused on web, mobile, and full-stack development using TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang. Critically, he has no demonstrated experience with industrial automation, IoT, message brokers, MQTT protocols, or the security frameworks (SDL, IEC 62443) that are core to the role.
strengths: 15+ years of software engineering experience exceeding the 8–12 year requirement, with clear progression to senior roles
Strong software craftsmanship mindset evidenced by TDD advocacy, mentoring initiatives, and conference talks on testing and web internals
Full-stack versatility across multiple languages and platforms (TypeScript, DLang, Java, C#) and recent experience with React and Node.js, overlapping with job's 'advantageous' skills
concerns: Minimal C++ expertise: only 6 months of professional C++ work at National Instruments a decade ago; recent 10-year career is TypeScript, DLang, and web-focused
Zero demonstrated experience in industrial automation, IoT, or embedded systems; career has centered on web platforms, media, investment tools, and GIS—incompatible with automation machinery domain
No evidence of security/compliance expertise: SDL practices and IEC 62443 industrial-cybersecurity standards (mandatory responsibilities) are completely absent from resume

key_factors.factor: Industrial automation and IoT experience gap
